skill.name: os-scripting
skill.description: Operating system and shell scripting troubleshooting workflow for Linux, macOS, and Windows. Covers bash scripting, system administration, debugging, and automation.
skill.summary: A comprehensive workflow bundle for OS troubleshooting, shell scripting, and system administration across Linux, macOS, and Windows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a documentation/workflow bundle skill (SKILL.md) that provides educational guidance on OS and shell scripting. All 93 static findings are false positives. The detected 'external_commands', 'network', and 'filesystem' patterns are example commands in documentation, not executable code. The skill's own metadata correctly identifies it as 'risk: safe'.

content.value_statement: This workflow bundle helps developers and system administrators debug shell scripts, create production-ready bash code, and automate system administration tasks across Linux, macOS, and Windows.
actual_capabilities: Diagnose system performance issues using standard Linux commands
Analyze and lint shell scripts with ShellCheck integration
Debug shell script errors using systematic techniques
Create production-ready bash scripts with error handling
Write automated tests using the Bats testing framework
Troubleshoot networks, disks, CPU, and memory issues
limitations: This is a workflow guide that invokes other skills, not a standalone execution engine
Requires other skills to be available (bash-linux, bash-pro, shellcheck-configuration, etc.)
Does not directly execute commands on the user's system
Assumes basic familiarity with command-line interfaces

use_cases.title: Debug a failing bash script
use_cases.description: Use systematic debugging techniques to trace and fix shell script errors with proper logging and error handling.

use_cases.title: Create production-ready automation
use_cases.description: Build robust shell scripts with proper error handling, input validation, and testing using Bats framework.

use_cases.title: Troubleshoot system performance
use_cases.description: Diagnose high CPU, memory issues, disk space problems, and network connectivity using standard system commands.

prompt_templates.title: Basic system diagnostics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @os-scripting to help me check system performance and identify any issues

prompt_templates.title: Shell script review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @os-scripting to analyze my shell script for issues and run ShellCheck on it

prompt_templates.title: Debug script errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @os-scripting to systematically debug this shell script that is failing with error: [describe error]

prompt_templates.title: Create automation script
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @os-scripting to help me create a production-ready backup script with error handling and tests

output_examples.input: Help me debug a bash script that exits with error code 1
output_examples.output: I'll help you debug this systematically. First, let me check the script for common issues using ShellCheck, then we'll enable debug mode and trace the execution...

output_examples.input: Create a monitoring script for disk space
output_examples.output: I'll create a production-ready monitoring script with proper error handling, logging, and notifications. This will check disk space and alert when usage exceeds thresholds...

output_examples.input: My server has high CPU usage, help troubleshoot
output_examples.output: Let's diagnose this systematically. We'll check running processes, identify resource-heavy tasks, and analyze system logs to find the cause...

best_practices: Always use 'set -euo pipefail' at the script start for strict error handling
Quote all variables to prevent word splitting and globbing issues
Add comprehensive logging with timestamps for troubleshooting
Write Bats tests for all critical script functionality
anti_patterns: Using unquoted variables that may contain spaces or special characters
Ignoring exit codes from commands with '|| true' without explicit handling
Not validating user input before using it in commands
Writing scripts without any error handling or logging

faq.question: What skills does this workflow bundle invoke?
faq.answer: This bundle orchestrates skills like bash-linux, bash-pro, shellcheck-configuration, systematic-debugging, bats-testing-patterns, and devops-troubleshooter.

faq.question: Can this skill execute commands on my system?
faq.answer: No, this is a documentation and workflow guide. It provides guidance and templates but does not directly execute shell commands.

faq.question: Do I need other skills installed for this to work?
faq.answer: Yes, this workflow bundle is designed to work with other skills like bash-linux, shellcheck-configuration, and bats-testing-patterns.

faq.question: What platforms does this support?
faq.answer: This workflow covers Linux, macOS, and Windows (via WSL or PowerShell equivalents).

faq.question: How do I get started with shell scripting?
faq.answer: Start with Phase 1 (Environment Assessment) to understand your system, then move to Phase 4 (Script Development) to create your first script.

faq.question: Can I use this for production automation?
faq.answer: Yes, the bundle includes quality gates and best practices for creating production-ready scripts with proper testing and error handling.
